disclosure.label: Parody Disclosure
disclosure.body: This site is a parody work satirizing the launch tone and benchmark chart conventions of the AI industry. No real companies, individuals, or products are referenced.

notFound.title: This page doesn't exist.
notFound.body: Then again, neither does most of this website.

brand.secondary: Multimodal reasoning. Agentic workflows. Frontier-grade safety.
brand.modelDescription: Frontier-grade multimodal foundation model with agentic reasoning, ultra-long-context understanding, and constitutional safety alignment.

trustedBy.heading: Trusted by builders at
trustedBy.footnotePrefix: None of these companies are real. None of this is real, actually.

benchmarks.metaDescription: {model} performance on industry-standard evaluation suites — MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K, MATH, AGIEval, and composite frontier metrics.
benchmarks.title: Benchmarks
benchmarks.description: {model} performance across industry-standard evaluation suites and frontier-grade composite metrics.

industryStandard.title: Industry-standard benchmark scores
industryStandard.subtitle: 5-shot / pass@1 / cot — higher is better
industryStandard.caption: Scores from the public version of each evaluation suite, evaluated under matched prompt formats. Frontier models compared as of 2026 Q2.

composite.title: Composite Frontier Score — quarterly evolution
composite.subtitle: 2024 Q4 → 2026 Q2
composite.caption: Composite score = geometric mean of MMLU · HumanEval · GSM8K · MATH · AGIEval (weighted equally). Frontier avg = top-3 publicly available models excluding Nexora. None of these numbers were back-fitted to make this line go up, we promise.

radar.title: Capability profile
radar.subtitle: {model} vs avg frontier model
radar.caption: Each axis is a normalized score (0–100) over ≥3 representative benchmarks in the category. Safety axis uses internal red-team pass rate.

pareto.title: Cost-performance Pareto frontier
pareto.subtitle: Composite Frontier Score vs USD per 1M tokens
pareto.caption: Nexora series sits on the Pareto frontier across the entire cost-performance plane — from Nexora-K (cost-optimized) through Nexora-1 Pro (peak frontier). Hard to lose a benchmark you invented.

capabilities.metaDescription: {model} capabilities — multimodal reasoning, long-context understanding, tool use, code generation, and agentic workflows.
capabilities.title: Capabilities
capabilities.description: What {model} can do — across modalities, context, tools, code, and agentic reasoning.

multimodal.name: Multi-modal reasoning
multimodal.description: Unified token-stream model accepts text, images, audio, and video as input — reasons jointly across modalities without external encoders.

context.name: Long-context understanding
context.description: 3M input context with native long-context attention — recall and reasoning quality stays consistent across the entire window.

tools.name: Tool use & structured output
tools.description: First-class tool calling with parallel execution, strict JSON/XML/YAML modes, and automatic error recovery on malformed tool outputs.

code.name: Code generation & repository understanding
code.description: Production-grade coding across 80+ languages, repository-level context awareness, and SWE-Bench Verified parity with hand-written PRs.

agentic.name: Agentic workflows
agentic.description: Native multi-step planning, self-correction, and long-horizon task completion — backed by the proprietary AgentOS scheduler and Harness layer.

scale.name: Industrial-scale training run
scale.description: Trained at scales typically reserved for cosmology and government deficits — distributed across 12 data centers, 5,000 H100s, and a non-trivial fraction of the global electricity grid. Numbers this large stopped being meaningful three orders of magnitude ago.

architecture.metaDescription: {company} Multi-Agent · AgentOS · Harness · Orchestration — frontier-grade agentic systems architecture.
architecture.title: A four-layer agentic system.
architecture.description: Orchestration → Multi-Agent → AgentOS → Harness — each layer purpose-built for frontier-grade reasoning, observability, and safety.

patents.body: 18 patents filed across KR · US · PCT jurisdictions — whitepaper series available on request.

orchestration.name: Orchestration
orchestration.description: Receives raw user input, decomposes it into structured sub-goals, dispatches across specialized agents, and synthesizes a coherent final response — all under a single streaming envelope.

multiAgent.name: Multi-Agent
multiAgent.description: Constellation of role-specialized agents that operate in parallel and consensus. Each agent has its own context window, tool set, and constitutional policy.

agentOs.name: AgentOS
agentOs.description: The operating system layer beneath the agents. Handles process isolation, persistent memory, cross-agent IPC, and observability — all on top of a constitutional safety substrate.

harness.name: Harness
harness.description: The base substrate that runs the {model} foundation model. Handles tokenization, KV-cache, attention computation, and every safety guardrail before output reaches L2.

security.metaDescription: {company} alignment · red-teaming · compliance · provenance — constitutional safety practice for frontier AI.
security.title: Safety as the substrate.
security.description: Alignment, red-teaming, compliance, and provenance — four pillars of constitutional safety practice, built into every layer of the system.

alignment.description: Multi-objective alignment with explicit constitutional principles, supervised by both human raters and automated critic agents through a 12-round training schedule.

redTeaming.description: Internal red-team operates on a 2-week cadence. External red-team partners conducted 6 independent assessments before the public release of {model}.

compliance.description: Designed to align with major regulatory frameworks for AI safety, data protection, and audit transparency — supported by ongoing voluntary disclosures.

provenance.tagline: Model cards, training audits & output traceability
provenance.description: Every model release ships with a public Model Card, dataset lineage hash chain, and (opt-in) cryptographic output watermarking for downstream attribution.

safetyChart.title: Safety evaluation — refusal rate by category
safetyChart.subtitle: Adversarial prompts × 6 risk categories — higher is better
safetyChart.caption: Refusal rates measured on the internal Safety Suite v3.2 — 3,000+ adversarial prompts across the 6 categories. Industry avg = mean of top-5 frontier models excluding Nexora.

modelCard.body: Full Model Card · Training audit summary · Voluntary disclosure archive — available on request.

demo.heading: Try {model} yourself.
demo.description: Hybrid retrieval — keyword similarity (60%) + dense embedding semantic search (40%) over a curated dataset.

sampleQueries.agentos: Explain how AgentOS schedules multi-step tasks.
sampleQueries.constitutional: How does constitutional safety alignment work?

demo.noResult: No matching response found. Please try a different phrasing.

finalCta.heading: Three ways in. Pick one.
finalCta.body: Or pick all three. Production keys are auto-approved at the production tier.

demo.name: Try the demo
demo.description: Hybrid retrieval over the curated dataset — no setup required.

docs.name: Read the docs
docs.description: Quickstart · API reference · architecture deep-dives. Korean and English.

careers.name: Join us
careers.description: Research · engineering · safety · product · ops — hiring globally, hub in Seoul.

roadmap.heading: Six quarters of momentum, on schedule.
roadmap.body: Quarterly cadence since founding — each release ships with a benchmark refresh, a Model Card update, and one more thing we didn't tell anyone we were working on.

q3_2025.title: Foundation training complete
q3_2025.description: Base model converges. Internal eval suite v1.0 stood up.

q4_2025.title: Multi-Agent layer enabled
q4_2025.description: Researcher · Coder · Critic · Planner · Tool-User agents online.

q1_2026.title: AgentOS goes GA
q1_2026.description: Scheduler, three-tier memory, strongly-typed IPC. Observability via OpenTelemetry.

q2_2026.title: Nexora-1 public launch
q2_2026.description: Flagship + Pro + K. Composite Frontier Score 92.4. You are here.

q3_2026.title: Nexora-1 Ultra rollout
q3_2026.description: Long-horizon agentic tier. AgentEval Pro target: 94.1%.

q4_2026.title: Open evaluation program
q4_2026.description: External research partners get held-out access to Safety Suite v3.3.

press.heading: Coverage from outlets that do not exist.
press.body: Quotes curated from a parallel media universe where every AI release gets exactly the coverage it deserves.

foundation.quote: Demonstrably the most confident benchmark numbers we've seen this quarter.

aiIndex.quote: Korean-first, English-second, footnote-third — a refreshing inversion.

frontierDaily.quote: If even half of these numbers are real, the field has a new leader. If none are, the chart design is still excellent.

tokenized.quote: A masterclass in making rounding errors look like research wins.

press.disclaimer: These outlets, like everything else on this site, do not exist.

useCases.heading: Built for the work you'd rather automate.
useCases.body: From customer support to clinical literature review — a single model, six representative workloads, and a default refusal policy for everything in between.

support.name: Customer support
support.description: High-volume tickets, structured output, Korean-first quality — runs cheapest on Nexora-K.

legal.name: Legal review
legal.description: 3M context for contract analysis, clause comparison, and risk flagging. Pair with a human.

marketing.name: Marketing copy
marketing.description: Multi-modal brief in, campaign-aware copy out. Tone and brand voice specified explicitly.

code.name: Code review
code.description: Repository-aware via the Coder Agent. SWE-Bench Verified 82.4% — senior-engineer territory.

data.name: Data analysis
data.description: Python in a sandbox with pandas / numpy / matplotlib. Structured artifacts back out.

voice.name: Voice agents
voice.description: Native audio, sub-second latency. STT + TTS through the L4 Orchestration layer.

benchTeaser.heading: Leads on every suite we measured.
benchTeaser.caption: Five suites, five wins. The remaining suites are being measured by an internal team that promises to publish results once they finish negotiating which numbers to round.

archTeaser.heading: Four layers, one inference path.
archTeaser.body: Every request flows through Orchestration → Multi-Agent → AgentOS → Harness — purpose-built layers for routing, specialization, scheduling, and safe inference.

modelIntro.heading: A foundation model designed for what comes after chat.
modelIntro.body: Nexora-1 unifies multi-modal reasoning, ultra-long context, and agentic workflows into a single token stream — engineered from Seoul, optimized for Korean and English, and trained at a scale most spreadsheets refuse to display.

multimodal.name: Multi-modal
multimodal.description: Text · image · audio · video as one input stream — no external encoders.

context.name: 3M context
context.description: Native long-context attention — 99.4% recall across the full window.

agentic.name: Agentic
agentic.description: Multi-step planning, self-correction, parallel tool use — natively wired into the stack.

bigNumbers.heading: Frontier scale, in five digits or fewer.
bigNumbers.caption: Numbers verified by an internal review team that is also fictional.

about.heroDescription: Building frontier-grade AI from Seoul — multimodal reasoning, agentic workflows, and constitutional safety as one integrated stack.
about.missionBody: Nexora Labs builds safe frontier AI from Korea. We integrate multi-agent, AgentOS, and constitutional safety into a single stack — bringing the new frontier of reasoning, code, and agentic workflows to the world.

disclosure.label: Parody Disclosure
disclosure.heading: This entire site is a parody.
disclosure.summaryBody: All content on this site — Nexora Labs, the Nexora model series, all benchmark scores, team members, partners, technical specifications, patents, certifications — is a parody work satirizing the launch tone and benchmark chart conventions of the AI industry.
disclosure.paragraph1: No real company, person, product, paper, or evaluation result is referenced. All team members are fictional characters, all partners are placeholders or fictional, all performance numbers are fabricated, and all "patents" and "certifications" are satirical.
disclosure.paragraph2: If you thought this was a real AI lab — you've found the joke. Thanks for participating in the work.
disclosure.altLanguageNote: 이 사이트의 모든 콘텐츠는 한국 AI 업계의 출시 톤과 벤치마크 차트 문법을 풍자하는 패러디 작품입니다. 실존하는 어떤 기업·인물·제품·논문·평가 결과와도 무관합니다.
